mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings contraction, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, producing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20-day returns, with the interaction term coefficient insignificant at p less than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity utilization tightening, which triggers short-term equity rallies as firms raise prices and margins.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during late-cycle phases consistently precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns, reversing the predicted direction.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0% annualized confirms sustainable aggregate demand, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% correlates with negative 60-day forward returns in greater than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4% monthly forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% monthly are followed by positive 5-day returns in greater than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms labor market tightening, which drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE shows zero predictive power for 20-day returns, with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal supply-side contraction that outweighs demand factors, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines are followed by positive 60-day returns in greater than 55% of instances.
